snippetLines.lineText: // A Mutex is a mutual exclusion lock.
// The zero value for a Mutex is an unlocked mutex.
// A Mutex must not be copied after first use.
// In the terminology of [the Go memory model],
// the n'th call to [Mutex.Unlock] “synchronizes before” the m'th call to [Mutex.Lock]
// A Mutex is a mutual exclusion lock.
// A Mutex provides mutual exclusion within and across processes by locking a
// well-known file. Such a file generally guards some other part of the
// filesystem: for example, a Mutex file in a directory might guard access to
// the entire tree rooted in that directory.
Path string     // The path to the well-known lock file. Must be non-empty.
mu   sync.Mutex // A redundant mutex. The race detector doesn't know about file locking, so in tests we may need to lock something that it understands.
// MutexAt returns a new Mutex with Path set to the given non-empty path.
func HammerMutex(m *Mutex, loops int, cdone chan bool) {
// the n'th call to [RWMutex.Unlock] “synchronizes before” the m'th call to Lock
// for any n < m, just as for [Mutex].
// For any call to RLock, there exists an n such that
w           Mutex        // held if there are pending writers
writerSem   uint32       // semaphore for writers to wait for completing readers
// Set up the runtime to always track casgstatus transitions for metrics.
// A rwmutex is a reader/writer mutual exclusion lock.
// The lock can be held by an arbitrary number of readers or a single writer.
// This is a variant of sync.RWMutex, for the runtime package.
// Like mutex, rwmutex blocks the calling M.
// It does not interact with the goroutine scheduler.
rLock      mutex    // protects readers, readerPass, writer
readers    muintptr // list of pending readers
// A zeroed Mutex is unlocked (no need to initialize each lock).
// Initialization is helpful for static lock ranking, but not required.
// totalMutexWaitTime is the sum of time goroutines have spent in _Gwaiting
// with a waitreason of the form waitReasonSync{RW,}Mutex{R,}Lock.
func blockMutexN(t *testing.T, n int, d time.Duration) {
// This binary collects a 1s delta mutex profile and dumps it to os.Stdout.
// This is in a subprocess because we want the base mutex profile to be empty
// (as a regression test for https://go.dev/issue/64566) and the only way to
// Disable the mutex profiler. This is the default, but that default is
// load-bearing for this test, which needs the base profile to be empty.
// the Unlock fast path should be inlined
// the Lock fast path should be inlined
if savedM, ok := cThreadToM[thread]; ok && savedM != m {
tmpl   map[string]*Template // Map from name to defined templates.
muFuncs    sync.RWMutex // protects parseFuncs and execFuncs
// File locking support for Plan 9. This uses fdMutex from the
done     atomic.Value          // of chan struct{}, created lazily, closed by first cancel call
// An atomic load is ~5x faster than a mutex, which can matter in tight loops.
// lines and infos are protected by mutex
lines []int // lines contains the offset of the first character for each line (the first entry is always 0)
mutex sync.RWMutex         // protects the file set
base  int                  // base offset for the next file
// sessionTicketKeys contains zero or more ticket keys. If set, it means
// writerMutex protects all KeyLogWriters globally. It is rarely enabled,
// and is only for debugging, so a global mutex saves space.
remain         int  // the number of bytes known to remain in the input
// Make sure we're single-threaded through here.
mutex      sync.Mutex              // each item must be sent atomically
w          []io.Writer             // where to send the data
mutex        sync.Mutex                              // each item must be received atomically
r            io.Reader                               // source of the data
// Make sure we're single-threaded through here.
// the response to find the original request ID.
// internal uint64 and save JSON on the side.
// and then look it up by request ID when filling out the rpc Response.
pending map[uint64]string // map request id to method name
F sync.Mutex // ERROR "undefined: sync|expected package|reference to undefined name"
sync.Mutex // ERROR "undefined: sync|expected package|reference to undefined name"
sync.Mutex // ERROR "undefined: sync|expected package|expected signature or type name|reference to undefined name"
mu     sync.Mutex // guards closed and list
